operating_state: Net sales increased 7.2% to $121.2 billion in Q2 2022, with AWS growing 33% while North America posted operating losses of $627 million versus $3.1 billion income in Q2 2021. International segment declined with $1.8 billion operating losses due to foreign exchange headwinds and normalized demand patterns. Overall operating income declined 57% to $3.3 billion as revenue growth was outpaced by operating expense increases across fulfillment network expansion and technology investments.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased to $117.9 billion driven by fulfillment network capacity additions and infrastructure investments. The company extended server depreciation lives from four to five years and networking equipment from five to six years, reducing Q2 depreciation expense by $928 million. Stock-based compensation increased while constrained labor markets elevated payroll costs across operations.
material_events: Amazon completed the MGM Holdings acquisition for $6.1 billion in March 2022 and executed a 20-for-1 stock split effective May 27, 2022. The company repurchased $6.0 billion of common stock during the first six months under new authorization and issued $12.8 billion in new long-term debt across seven tranches with maturities from 2024 to 2062. David H. Clark resigned as CEO Worldwide Consumer effective July 1, 2022.
macro_exposures: Foreign exchange volatility created $3.5 billion revenue headwinds with continued exposure to major currency fluctuations. The company recorded $11.5 billion in valuation losses on Rivian equity investment during the first six months, including $3.9 billion in Q2 2022. Global supply chain constraints, constrained labor markets, and inflation pressures on fuel, energy, and commodities elevated operational costs and reduced fulfillment network productivity.
forward_outlook: Amazon entered an agreement to acquire One Medical for $3.9 billion in July 2022, subject to customary closing conditions. Third quarter 2022 guidance projects net sales between $125.0-130.0 billion representing 13-17% growth with 390 basis points unfavorable foreign exchange impact, while operating income is expected between $0-3.5 billion compared to $4.9 billion in prior year.
uncertainty_profile: Patent infringement lawsuits present potential damages ranging from $130-250 million to $166-986 million across multiple cases, with additional recent litigation seeking unspecified damages. Tax contingencies total $3.3 billion with ongoing IRS examinations for 2016 and thereafter, while the European Commission's Luxembourg tax ruling case remains under appeal. Regulatory investigations continue across European marketplace operations focusing on data usage and competitive practices.
